meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: End Project Bloat with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Master a high-velocity client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship premium SaaS apps at record speed.
content.answer_block: To stop leaving projects unfinished, you must shift from manual coding to an AI-orchestrated pipeline. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you treat your codebase as a product, not a chore. This workflow enforces modularity, automates repetitive boilerplate, and keeps client deliverables on schedule.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial architecture is brilliant, the tech stack is bleeding-edge, and the client is ecstatic. But three weeks in, the momentum dies. Features sprawl, technical debt mounts, and the finish line retreats into a fog of half-baked commits and mounting anxiety. You are not failing as a developer; you are failing at the architecture of delivery.

It is time to stop 'coding' and start 'engineering outcomes.' By treating your development process like a high-performance CI/CD pipeline, you can eliminate the friction that causes projects to stall. We are moving beyond simple text editors into an era of AI-driven synthesis, where your role shifts from typist to architect. Let's rebuild your workflow.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Feature Set
steps.description: Break down the client requirements into strictly defined, isolated features to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these client requirements. Break them into 5-10 atomic, testable features with clear acceptance criteria. Output in JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Analyze requirements and generate a feature-list.json file'

steps.title: Scaffold the Modular Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, decoupled directory structure that scales with feature additions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure for a Next.js/Supabase SaaS. Ensure modularity for future feature additions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Scaffold the project directory based on the generated architecture'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Eliminate repetitive tasks by letting the AI handle standard API routes and database schemas.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate CRUD API routes and database migration files for the following schema: [Insert Schema].
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Implement the generated CRUD routes into the project source'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation Cycles
steps.description: Use Claude Code to execute feature implementation in 30-minute high-focus sprints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review current code state. Implement the 'Auth' feature according to the acceptance criteria defined in Step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Complete the Auth feature implementation and run unit tests'

steps.title: Automated Quality Gateways
steps.description: Ensure code integrity before delivery by automating the testing and linting process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD configuration to run unit tests and linting on every push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Setup GitHub Actions for automated testing and linting'

pro_tips: Never start a session without a clear 'Definition of Done' for the current feature.
Use Claude Code to generate 'self-documenting' code comments that explain the 'why' behind complex architectural decisions.
Keep your context window clean by clearing temporary files and stale logs before starting a new feature branch.
Treat your client's initial feedback as a set of constraints rather than a moving target; update your Antigravity prompt to reflect these new constraints immediately.
Always run a final 'Refactor for Readability' pass using AI after a feature is completed to ensure the code remains maintainable for future updates.
content.next_steps: Integrate this workflow into your next client project and track your 'Time to First Feature' metric. Iterate on your prompts based on the specific needs of your client's tech stack.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting projects drift into the abyss of unfinished work. Master the tools that professional AI Builders use to ship consistently and profitably. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
